content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

March 20, 1985

John E. Buril, President
Sunshine, Linda Vista, American
River Mobilehome Association
7721 Auburn Blvd., Space 25
Citrus Heights, CA 95610

Re: Our File No. A-85-041

Dear Mr. Buril:

     This is in response to your letter of February 11, 1985,
regarding the campaign disclosure and conflict of interest pro-
visions of the Political Reform Act (Government Code Sections
81000-91014).

     First, you requested information with respect to three
registered campaign committees--Citizens for Better Housing,
the Pro Tem 25 Committee, and the Affordable Housing Association.
Any committee which receives or spends $500 in a calendar year
for political purposes must file a Statement of Organization
(Form 410) with the Office of the Secretary of State. The
Form 410 contains information regarding the committee's purpose,
affiliations, etc., and you may obtain copies of Statements of
Organization by contacting the Office of the Secretary of State,
Political Reform Division, 1230 J Street, Sacramento (phone
322-4880). In addition, if a committee is active on a statewide
basis, campaign statements disclosing its receipts and expendi-
tures will be on file and available for inspection with the
Secretary of State.

     With respect to your second question, I am enclosing a copy
of the Commission's Guide to California's Conflict of Interest
Law, which I hope will be helpful in explaining when a public
official must disqualify himself or herself from participating
in a decision affecting a personal financial interest. Generally
speaking, however, an official would not be required to abstain
from voting on a matter affecting the source of gifts with an
aggregate value of less than $250 per calendar year.

John E. Buril
March 20, 1985
Page 2


I hope this information is helpful.  Please let me know if
you need further assistance.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Carla J. Wardlow
                                        Political Reform Consultant

7721 Auburn Blvd., Space 25
Citrus Heights, CA 95610
February 11, 1985                        FEB 13 1 40 PM 35




Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Attn: Director

Dear Sir:

In examining some of the disbursement records available to us under the Campaign
Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act, I find the following listed
anonymous receipts of fairly substantial amounts of money from the Mobilehome
Parks Political Action Committee, a controlled subsidiary of Western Mobilehome
Association. The ones of interest are:

1. Citizens for Better Housing, ID#830672
2. The Pro Tem 25 Committee, ID#830602
3. The Affordable Housing Association, ID#82292

I wonder if you would advise us please, of the rest of the registration informa-
tion for these identification numbers. The names of the recipients are in-
adequate to understand who they really represent or what their political
objectives might be. I would appreciate any information you can furnish us.

I also have a question: What is your position as the Fair Political Practices
Commission with respect to an elected official receiving cash money as birthday
presents. In this case the amount was $100. or more and the individual recipient
that I have reference to has no hesitation in voting on matters of interest to
the giver of the birthday gift. I would be interested in knowing if this
individual should not have disqualified themselves in these circumstances.
Your comments with respect to this elected official receiving birthday presents
will be of interest.

Very truly yours,

John E. Buril, President
Sunshine, Londa Vista, American River Mobilehome Assn.

JEB:sh

sections.question: First, you requested information with respect to three registered campaign committees--Citizens for Better Housing, the Pro Tern 25 Committee, and the Affordable Housing Association. Any committee which receives or spends $500 in a calendar year for political purposes must file a Statement of Organization (Form 410) with the Office of the Secretary of State. With respect to your second question, I am enclosing a copy of the Commission's Guide to California's Conflict of Interest Law, which I hope will be helpful in explaining when a public official must disqualify himself or herself from participating in a decision affecting a personal financial interest.
sections.conclusion: Generally speaking, however, an official would not be required to abstain from voting on a matter affecting the source of gifts with an aggregate value of less than $250 per calendar year.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks for registration information on three campaign committees that received substantial anonymous disbursements, and seeks FPPC guidance on whether an elected official who receives cash birthday gifts ($100+) from interested parties must disqualify themselves from voting on matters affecting the gift giver.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that an official is not required to disqualify themselves from voting on matters affecting sources of gifts valued under $250 per calendar year. The Commission directs the requestor to the Secretary of State's office for campaign committee registration information and provides a conflict of interest law guide for reference.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (February 11, 1985) and FPPC response letter (March 20, 1985). Response letter is primary document. Question sections extracted from response narrative rather than formal headers. Conclusion synthesized from relevant guidance in response body. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present.

QUESTION: First, you requested information with respect to three registered campaign committees--Citizens for Better Housing, the Pro Tern 25 Committee, and the Affordable Housing Association. Any committee which receives or spends $500 in a calendar year for political purposes must file a Statement of Organization (Form 410) with the Office of the Secretary of State. With respect to your second question, I am enclosing a copy of the Commission's Guide to California's Conflict of Interest Law, which I hope will be helpful in explaining when a public official must disqualify himself or herself from participating in a decision affecting a personal financial interest.

CONCLUSION: Generally speaking, however, an official would not be required to abstain from voting on a matter affecting the source of gifts with an aggregate value of less than $250 per calendar year.

embedding.summary: FPPC response to John E. Buril regarding campaign disclosure requirements for three committees and conflict of interest rules concerning elected officials receiving birthday gifts. The Commission provides guidance on gift thresholds and directs him to the Secretary of State for committee registration details.
